prereg_falsifier: T3 source-only transport SUCCEEDS iff Spearman(predicted_gap, true_gap) across held-out donors > 0.5 AND mean|pred-true| < 0.03. T2 anchor-recal SUCCEEDS iff coverage at anchor f=0.20 is within 0.03 of nominal. Declared before run.

T1_gap_estimability.ruling: MODERATE: gap is a partly-stable quantity (corr 0.64 identity) but noisy; ridge arm unstable (corr -0.33).

T2_anchor_recalibration.ruling: SUCCESS. A 5% target-donor labeled anchor restores coverage to 0.90 (gap -0.002); 10%->+0.001; 20%->-0.004. THE DEPLOYABLE ANSWER: recalibration needs a handful of anchor perturbations, not the whole donor labeled.

T3_source_only_transport.ruling: FAILS (falsified vs pre-declared Spearman>0.5, |err|<0.03). Source-only Spearman -0.26 (worse than random); cannot forecast WHICH donor undercovers from source donors alone. Per-donor gaps (-0.045..+0.093) do not transport label-free at n=6.

honest_headline: The red-team's objection is CONFIRMED for the label-free case: you cannot predict a specific unseen donor's coverage gap from source donors alone (Spearman -0.26, n=6). BUT the deployable fix is cheap: a 5% labeled anchor from the target donor restores exact 0.90 coverage. The trust-layer claim is corrected from 'the gap transports for free' to 'a small anchor recalibrates it' — an honest, actionable protocol with a pre-registered falsifier.
merit_reading: Net-positive for CREDIBILITY, neutral for TIER. Replaces a broken claim with a working, pre-registered one, and shows the project falsifies its own hypotheses. n=6 donors caps the transport power (can't rule out transport with more donors). Honest merit stays 78; this is exactly the rigor that consolidates the tier, not a jump.
